_description: Mudmat / foundation structure database for GTM demo 3 (installation analysis). Three representative sizes covering typical subsea infrastructure foundations.
_references: DNV-RP-H103 (2011) - Modelling and Analysis of Marine Operations, Sec 4 (Lifting through wave zone)
DNV-ST-N001 (2021) - Marine Operations and Marine Warranty
DNV-RP-C205 (2010) - Environmental Conditions and Environmental Loads

constants._note: Submerged weight = (mass_air - displaced_water_mass) * g. Mudmats are open-framed steel structures with flat bottom plate.

structures.name: Mudmat-S-50te
structures.description: Small mudmat foundation for manifold protection structure or single well template

mass_properties._note: Displaced volume estimated as 10% of bounding box (open frame structure)

centre_of_gravity._note: Relative to geometric centre of bottom plate

hydrodynamic_coefficients._note: Cs=5.0 for flat bottom slamming per DNV-RP-H103 Table 4-1. Cd for sharp-edged flat plate. Ca=1.0 for flat plate (added mass = rho * volume * Ca).

projected_areas._note: Bottom area = L x W = 6 x 6. Side areas are net projected areas accounting for open frame.

structures.name: Mudmat-M-100te
structures.description: Medium mudmat foundation for production manifold or PLET cluster

centre_of_gravity._note: Relative to geometric centre of bottom plate

hydrodynamic_coefficients._note: Same coefficients as small mudmat - similar flat-bottom geometry

structures.name: Mudmat-L-200te
structures.description: Large mudmat foundation for major manifold or multi-well template

centre_of_gravity._note: Relative to geometric centre of bottom plate

hydrodynamic_coefficients._note: Same coefficients - flat bottom geometry dominates

rigging._note: 4-point lift with heavy spreader frame. May require custom rigging design.
